spec_match.rationale: Covers part of the spec (1 of 3 named API surfaces) — distinct angle worth picking up.

interactive.rationale: Real controls + script-driven side-effects make this hands-on, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Page covers the promises the feature index makes for this concept.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is covered but only a few failure modes are surfaced.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link as required.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex, button overrides keep bg+color paired.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for csspseudoelement-support-for-backdrop-scroll-marker-and-view-transitions
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: Extends CSSPseudoElement interface to ::backdrop, ::scroll-marker, and view-transition pseudos (::view-transition, ::view-transition-group, ::view-transition-image-pair, ::view-transition-old, ::view-transition-new). Accessible via Element.pseudo() / getComputedStyle. Edge cases: ::backdrop only exists for top-layer elements; ::scroll-marker only on scroll-marker-group items; view-transition pseud...

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently shows the happy path. Add a UI affordance that drives the feature into a failure mode the spec explicitly calls out (timeout, denied permission, cross-origin block, malformed input) and surface what the browser actually does.

summary: Workable concept with a couple of gaps. 2 open question(s) queued.
